news.title: Art in Focus: Q&A with MFA candidate Mark Almanza
news.summary_en: Mark Almanza is an interdisciplinary artist who works across a variety of mediums to explore their Chicano identity. Their MFA thesis exhibition, “Don’t Everybody Like The Smell Of Gasoline?” will be on view April 21–26 in UW–Madison’s Art Lofts Gallery.
news.original_title: Art in Focus: Q&A with MFA candidate Mark Almanza
news.summary_cn: Mark Almanza is an interdisciplinary artist who works across a variety of mediums to explore their Chicano identity. Their MFA thesis exhibition, “Don’t Everybody Like The Smell Of Gasoline?” will be on view April 21–26 in UW–Madison’s Art Lofts Gallery.

news.title: UW–Madison research reveals experiences of HMoob (Hmong) undergraduates across Universities of Wisconsin
news.summary_en: An updated statistical portrait of HMoob (Hmong) American undergraduates enrolled in the 13 campuses of the Universities of Wisconsin reveals a college experience marked by unique challenges and opportunities.
news.original_title: UW–Madison research reveals experiences of HMoob (Hmong) undergraduates across Universities of Wisconsin

news.title: Tandem Press honored by Print Center New York
news.summary_en: UW–Madison’s Tandem Press is being honored for its significant contributions to the field of printmaking at Print Center New York's spring benefit on May 11.
news.original_title: Tandem Press honored by Print Center New York

news.title: UW–Madison’s Shanahan co-authors feature article in SDC Journal
news.summary_en: UW–Madison’s Ann M. Shanahan, a professor of acting and directing in the School of Education’s Department of Theatre and Drama, is the co-author of a feature article in the winter 2026 issue of SDC Journal’s peer-reviewed section.
